reviews.review_title: The game is so broken and too hard
reviews.review_description: There’s no way to actually get the full version. You can’t even play the game because literally if one animal sees you even if you get so far away that they shouldn’t be able to track you the animal can still find you and kill you and there’s no way to chop trees down to get lumber to make a crafting table to make a sword because for some reason the trees won’t fell, oh, and before you say, the owners are working on it they stopped development for it, even though they know the game is still broken
The tutorial is horrible, it explains absolutely nothing and it is incredibly broken

Good game. A little janky, but bearable. Just add a couple physics/graphics/multiplayer updates, and you got yourself an amazing game - amazing concept btw. It's an open world survival - like Minecraft or Red Dead. Developers, add a few updates, and you're looking at the top VR game. Good job; excited to see what y'all come up with!

First off, game is incredible. Love the slow, grindy feel of the game. But it desperately needs multiplayer, building a base with your friend or going hunting together would be sick and you could have things that require 2 or more players. Like certain buildings etc.

I’ve been playing this game for a while, if you like survival based games and slow paced “grindy” type of games this is it. Though I am left with many questions like do you beat the game once you reach the floating island and acend or is there something else I’m supposed to do. I played it on pcvr and meta, theres no difference. And the creator has many YT vids of him playing premortal but when i go looking up what to do next im left puzzled. He shows many islands,monsters, armours, and weapons. Im so confused

Been playin in for hours and possibly one of my first survival game ive played. It was hard at first but after i get the hang of the crafting and building system and destroyed the first boss(a frickin giant BOAR!) im hooked. After that i unlocked more crafting stuff and enjoying my bow and arrow and hunt me some deers. im on my way to the next boss,possibly a giant Deer. 

The problem i encounter is the save.Sometime it doesn't wanna save!!so i have to exit the game and restart it.Annoying but still playable.The other is when i load it,i clipped inside the ocean or a hill,so have to walk until i un"clipped" myself,fortunately. Really hope the dev fix this. Maybe then i'll give it a 5 stars
i love how the game is so realistic but also me and my brother would like a multiplayer

DISCLAIMER FOR ALL!: the game has a duplication glitch. I tried to craft a pickaxe, but when I canceled it while holding a crafted part, it let me keep the part, and gave me my materials I used to make the canceled craft. Also, the thing used to cook meat sometimes is already built a little when placing it.

THOUGHTS:The game is very realistic and is a blast. The game has manual crafting, doing steps, and converting wood into planks. The game does something that no other game has, with types of wood (big planks, small planks, twigs, sticks, logs, branches) and the day and night cycle being long.

It's a great game and all, I love the crafting and the way you manually have to make your tools and buildings. it just needs a little bit better graphics, and it could be the best game ever!

The game is okay for playing for a while. A lot of crafting and interesting mechanics.

When I saw that this game was not only a survival but a fantasy AND it was in VR I was like “sign me the flip up”. On a more serious note, this game is amazing. I love the crafting and resource gathering. One minor detail which is more of a skill issue but I can’t for the life of me throw my spear and hit the dang rabbit ever. I want to be clothed! I feel ashamed of myself when I walk past a boar and he has to see me naked.
Good game over all I find the graphics not the best but it's free so that's not the worst and durability sucks bosses have a lot of HP basic things are hard to craft but overall it's a good game

Very frustrating... the bow needs a lot work. It takes to many tools to make basic stuff because their durability is so low. I can see how it will be a great game but it still needs a lot of work. The boss's have way to much hp and take forever to kill and there's no way level your character stats other than eating food which wears off kinda quick. It would be a lot better if you could play with other people to help with everything.

Cons:

Graphics are terrible.

Low weapon durability.

Bad hand tracking for picking up items and throwing.

Hard bow mechanics.

If you accidentally scare deer into the ocean they will not gravitate back towards spawn and will become stuck.

Hard smithing mechanics.

Game is abandoned.

-----------

Pros:

It's free.

Unique crafting system.

Yo, if you’re looking for a game better then a township tale in the survival aspect ￼it’s definitely this one, it’s a little confusing to get the hang of and figure things out but it’s fun once you have it down, also I love how you can stack logs without them rolling away and the log sled actually keeps the logs in it🤩, It took me a bit to figure out how to sprint but like I said once you figure it out it’s fun.    ￼￼p.s, in the beginning it’s easier to kill the board with two axes👌👌

you can never lose hostile animals like boars so you end up running away for like 30 mins only to look behind you and them still chasing you. i legit did a lap of a fairly large island only to try to lose them in water, guess what? they will swim to you. u just cant lose the dam things

Let's be real: first thing you're going to get from this is a Valenheim clone sort of feel but VR, which, to be fair, I'm not altogether displeased with.  I'd actually like to see that.  For real.  But this one is quite young yet.  The graphics are basic and the worldbuilding is pretty sparse... but what we do have here is an in-depth, visceral and intuitive crafting system that I have not seen in any other VR game so far.  It's a little weird in spots (using a hammer on wedges to carve a handle and i can't just chop up the logs and branches for firewood with my axe for some reason) and it actually takes a bit of work to get the forging right.  I aknowledge that it might not be for everybody but I absolutly love it.  I want to see more of it.  Not just here but throughout the industry, so i will gladly support this effort.  
This one could be big and i genuinely hope it does keep going.  I want to see more builds, iron and steel tools, bigger worlds, NPCs and trading, more casting recipes.  I also hope they'll take a page from The Long Dark and lean to a more stylized graphics pallette rather than get hung up on realism and keep focusing on gameplay.
This one could go far... or it could sputter out before it realy gets a chance but I'm hopeful for it's potential and looking forward to seeing more.

This game does not have the best graphics but more importantly it has progressive unlocks and it features evolution which provides many hours of satisfying game play. As you play and progress there is a feeling of accomplishment which a lot of vr games lack.
Its so good but the rabbits are so Hard to catch so pls fix that.

More updates could make this an amazing game, but atm it is very mid. Kinda laggy, random game breaking bugs (sleeping in bed broke my game completely twice), small bugs that are very noticeable (weapon falling out of hand with no reason for it to as an example) As it stands now, better choices out there.

When I first started, I enjoyed it a lot, despite my initial stupidity. Upon reading the guide section of the handbook, I was glad to find actual information that saved me plenty of time. There are a few issues however: 1. Throwing physics is not this game's forte, trying to throw the spear or Tuskerang isn't all that good and the Tuskerang often gets stuck mid-air. 2. The bow physics are also messy, for some reason when I draw back the string of the bow, it begins to shake and tremble as if I'm freaking out holding a gun. The arrows themselves will often drop and waste durability if the arrowhead hits a solid surface, such as when you have a pouch of two. 3. Lastly, the grip isn't very good, even when cranked to 0.99. It simply drops your weapon, which isn't good when fighting Antleran or Fangan. Overall, decent game, just needs a bit of polishing.
Game is unfinished and the discord is dead

I was so excited when I saw that there was a survival game for free. I love playing this game for its creative part. Don't get me wrong, survival is even better. But there are times when I just want to build and not be attacked. 

Why I gave this game three stars: I would do so much in it, build the house, build items, put up my torches, fencing, bridges, furniture, etc. But when I log back in the only thing that's there is the house and the fence. Sooo much work out the window. That's frustrating. If that is fixed then I'll come back and change to five stars because that's what this game deserves. 

Other issues that don't really bother me:
Whenever I set the play mode to 'sitting' the character is still slightly squatting, from my tallest character to the shortest one (1.6). It makes it hard to run when needed.
• Sometimes when I log back in I respawn in the ocean. And the closest island isn't even the island I set up shop at. (Which is why I started building bridges, but when I log out the bridge is gone sometimes).

UPDATE: now the game just isn't saving. I'd hit the save button in the book and nothing. Not even the notification of 'game saved' comes up.

reviews.review_title: better then a lot of survival games
reviews.review_description: Has a very intuitive crafting system that I haven’t seen before, very interesting bosses, and an overall fun theme. The only bugs I found were that bows don’t fire right sometimes, and that the game stops saving after properly after a day or two of playing. It doesnt go back either, at all, the bug becomes permanent, and the game stops saving every 4 minutes when I log back into the world. Deleting and reinstalling doesn’t help, neither does pretty much anything else.

This game is amazing BUT has one major flaw, not the bugs but it’s gameplay I wanna play with friends and not by myself because sometimes I get lonely in the wild so it would be nice to play with friends
